# Summary
<!--
  Written by the dev. 2–5 sentences.
  Answer: what does this PR do and why? What is the user-facing or system-level behavior that changed?
  Save implementation details for "What Changed?" below.
-->

## What Changed?
<!--
  Summarize the diff or commit log into subsections below.
  Group changes as vertical slices — each subsection should represent a coherent area of the codebase
  (e.g. a feature, a layer, a shared utility, a config concern) rather than individual files.
  Use specific names: column names, function names, type names, endpoint paths, file names.
  Do not editorialize or repeat yourself across sections. Remove any subsection that has nothing to report.

  NOTE: The subsections below are just examples — rename, add, or remove them to match this PR.
-->

### [e.g. Database / Migrations]
<!--
  Schema changes, model methods, nullability, constraints, FK relationships, column renames.
  Note behavioral implications (e.g. "decoupled from X so historical records are preserved").
-->



### [e.g. Seeding]
<!--
  New seed files, reorganized structure, new reference data, changed conventions.
  If the seeding strategy changed, explain the new convention so future devs know where to add things.
-->



### [e.g. Feature or Use Case Name]
<!--
  New utilities, refactored logic, changed return types, new side effects, before/after behavior.
-->



### [e.g. API / Shared Schema]
<!--
  Request/response shape changes, renamed fields, validation schema updates.
  Call out any downstream consumers that may need updates.
-->



### [e.g. Config / Tooling]
<!--
  tsconfig, env handling, build config, lint rules.
  Note if a change fixes a silent failure mode or IDE false-positives.
-->



---

## Testing
<!--
  Describe what tests changed in this branch: new tests added, existing tests updated, tests removed and why.
  If no tests changed, briefly note how the changes were verified manually.
-->

## Out of Scope / Follow-up Tickets
<!--
  Anything intentionally deferred or triggered in another repo.
  Format: "[ ] Short description — PAY-### (This looks wrong in VS Code markdown, but will format correctly to a checkbox in GitHub PR Summary)
  Delete this section if there is nothing to note.

  IMPORTANT: Follow-up tickets need to exist in JIRA before it gets listed here.
  Confirm with the Team if a follow-up ticket should exist.
-->

- [ ] Short description - PAY-###
